| Overview  * [Historical persecution of Christians](/wiki/Persecution_of_Christians "Persecution of Christians") * [Catholic Church persecutions 1939–1958](/wiki/Persecutions_of_the_Catholic_Church_and_Pius_XII "Persecutions of the Catholic Church and Pius XII") * [Eradication of the Church under Stalinism](/wiki/Pope_Pius_XII_and_Russia "Pope Pius XII and Russia") * [Eastern Catholic persecutions](/wiki/Eastern_Catholic_victims_of_Soviet_persecutions "Eastern Catholic victims of Soviet persecutions") * [Persecution of Christians in the modern era](/wiki/Persecution_of_Christians_in_the_modern_era "Persecution of Christians in the modern era")

| * [v](/wiki/Template:Persecutions_of_the_Catholic_Church_sidebar "Template:Persecutions of the Catholic Church sidebar") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Persecutions_of_the_Catholic_Church_sidebar "Template talk:Persecutions of the Catholic Church sidebar") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Persecutions_of_the_Catholic_Church_sidebar "Special:EditPage/Template:Persecutions of the Catholic Church sidebar")

[SJ](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 邓以明; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 鄧以明; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Dèng Yǐmíng*; [Jyutping](/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping"): *dang6 ji5 ming4*; May 13, 1908 – June 27, 1995) was a Chinese [Jesuit](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") priest. Appointed [Bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") in 1951 and later [archbishop of Canton](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Canton "Archbishop of Canton"), he spent twenty-two years in jail for his loyalty to the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_church "Roman Catholic church") and died in exile in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").

**Dominic Tang Yee-ming** [SJ](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 邓以明; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 鄧以明; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Dèng Yǐmíng*; [Jyutping](/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping"): *dang6 ji5 ming4*; May 13, 1908 – June 27, 1995) was a Chinese [Jesuit](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") priest. Appointed [Bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") in 1951 and later [archbishop of Canton](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Canton "Archbishop of Canton"), he spent twenty-two years in jail for his loyalty to the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_church "Roman Catholic church") and died in exile in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").

## Jesuit and Bishop

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dominic_Tang&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Jesuit and Bishop")]

Tang was born in 1908[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 in [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") and decided to enter the [Jesuit](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") [novitiate](/wiki/Novitiate "Novitiate") in Spain in August 1930. Back in China, he studied [Catholicism](/wiki/Catholicism "Catholicism") in [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai"). Tang was ordained a priest in 1941.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 After his ordination he worked as a parish priest, principal of a primary school and did social welfare work in the [Ecclesiastical Province of Guangzhou](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Guangzhou "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Guangzhou").[[4]](#cite_note-Kung_foundation-4) [Pope Pius XII](/wiki/Pope_Pius_XII "Pope Pius XII") appointed him on 1 October 1950 as [Apostolic Administrator](/wiki/Apostolic_Administrator "Apostolic Administrator") of Canton ([Guangzhou](/wiki/Guangzhou "Guangzhou")), and on 13 February 1951 he was [ordained](/wiki/Ordination "Ordination") [titular bishop](/wiki/Titular_bishop "Titular bishop") of [Elateia](/wiki/Elateia "Elateia") by Bishop Gustave Deswaziere, who said of him: "*By accepting the appointment from the Holy See in these difficult times, the new bishop was showing absolute obedience and a spirit of sacrifice."*[[4]](#cite_note-Kung_foundation-4)

## Imprisonment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dominic_Tang&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Imprisonment")]

Tang was arrested in 1958.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 The [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China") charged him as "the most faithful running-dog of the reactionary [Vatican](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See")". He remained in jail for 22 years in [Laogai prison](/wiki/Laogai "Laogai") because he refused to sever contact with the [Pope](/wiki/Pope "Pope"), as the government ordered him to.

In 1987, he released his book *How Inscrutable His Ways!* In it he summarized his attitudes while incarcerated for 22 years:

* *"In prison, I always asked God to grant me the grace to progress in virtue., e.g. humility and obedience....I obeyed only the regulations which did not conflict with the principles of my faith. I want to be gentle and kind to others, without resisting ill-treatment from others; when controlled and walked on, I did not complain. There are many opportunities for practicing virtue in prison."*
* "*When I was a seminarian, I learned to do God's will. God's will required me to practice virtue in prison. This was God's love for me."[[5]](#cite_note-5)*

His sudden release (on June 2, 1980)[[6]](#cite_note-6) was due to a developing cancer, he then was given permission to leave the People's Republic of China for a cancer operation in [Hong Kong](/wiki/British_Hong_Kong "British Hong Kong").[[4]](#cite_note-Kung_foundation-4)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Later years

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dominic_Tang&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Later years")]

In 1980, the Guangzhou [Chinese Catholic Patriotic Association](/wiki/Chinese_Catholic_Patriotic_Association "Chinese Catholic Patriotic Association") affirmed Tang's status as bishop.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85

Later in 1980, Tang met with [Agostino Casaroli](/wiki/Agostino_Casaroli "Agostino Casaroli") in Hong Kong, as part of an effort by the [Holy See and the People's Republic of China](/wiki/China%E2%80%93Holy_See_relations "China–Holy See relations") to improve relations.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 84–85 At the time, both the Vatican and the China viewed Tang favorably.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85

In May 1981, at the age of 73, he was appointed [Archbishop](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of Canton (Guangzhou).[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 The Chinese government contended that his appointment as Archbishop went against the principles of the patriotic church's independence.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 On 22 June 1981, China's [Religious Affairs Bureau](/wiki/Religious_Affairs_Bureau "Religious Affairs Bureau") removed him from his position as an authorized bishop.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85

Tang was not permitted to return to China.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 After spending several years in Hong Kong, he went to the United States.[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85 He died in [Stamford, Connecticut](/wiki/Stamford,_Connecticut "Stamford, Connecticut") at the age of 87 and was buried at [Mission Santa Clara de Asís](/wiki/Mission_Santa_Clara_de_As%C3%ADs "Mission Santa Clara de Asís") in [Santa Clara, California](/wiki/Santa_Clara,_California "Santa Clara, California").[[3]](#cite_note-:10-3): 85
